items.q: What is hexadecimal A in denary?
items.why: Students forget hex uses A-F to represent 10-15 because we need 16 distinct symbols. A=10, B=11, C=12, D=13, E=14, F=15.

items.q: Why is UDP used for live video streaming despite being unreliable?
items.correct: Lower overhead and latency - retransmitting late packets would harm real-time quality.
items.misconception: Because TCP cannot handle video data.
items.random_wrong: Because UDP is always faster than TCP.
items.why: Students assume reliability is always desirable. For real-time media, a dropped packet is preferable to a delayed one - UDP trades reliability for timeliness, which suits live media.

items.q: What does an INNER JOIN return?
items.correct: Only rows with matching values in both tables.
items.misconception: All rows from both tables combined.
items.random_wrong: Only the inner columns of each table.
items.why: Students confuse INNER (intersection) with FULL OUTER (union). INNER drops unmatched rows from BOTH sides; LEFT keeps all from left; RIGHT keeps all from right; FULL OUTER keeps everything.

items.q: Which gate needs both inputs true?
items.why: The answer examiners want is: AND. “OR” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: How many rows does a truth table with 2 inputs have?
items.why: The real figure is 4. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
